themes.exampleQuote: Not just your normal sugary affair but elevated with unique flavor and texture.
Amazing stop for a date night or after dinner.
They've always had issues regarding speed of service. We wound up leaving both times.
Gluten and nut free options are fire.
Awesome baked goods but really needs a quality and service check on coffee.
A great place to meet up with a friend, have a specialty coffee, and talk for hours.

notable_quote: You can tell a lot of time and love went into crafting those recipes. They should be proud.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently praise the inventive baked goods and warm atmosphere, but a recurring friction around coffee quality and slow service lines keeps the sentiment from climbing higher.
quarter_narrative: Twenty Pittsburgh creators posted TikToks tagged at Butterwood this quarter, generating nearly 600,000 plays, while the business itself has not posted on Instagram in over 500 days. The pastries are moving through feeds city-wide without the kitchen saying a word. Meanwhile, Allegheny Coffee and Tea Exchange holds the family lead on the back of social momentum, a signal Butterwood demonstrably has the product to compete on but is not converting into its own presence. A return to posting, even sporadically, would be enough to move this rank; the creator infrastructure is already in place.
tldr_read: Nearly 600,000 TikTok plays from 20 creators, zero posts of its own in 515 days. Ones to Watch, #2 in Pittsburgh Cafes.
tldr_meaning: The Instagram dormancy is the only axis holding this business out of the Icons conversation; every other signal, visual catalog, creator fit, conversion path, review volume, points upward.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Twenty Creators, Six Hundred Thousand Plays, And Butterwood Has Not Posted In 515 Days.

playbook.headline: Restart the Instagram account now
playbook.action: A single post showing the current pastry case would end a 515-day silence and reactivate a 3.92% engagement rate that still exists.

playbook.headline: Fix the coffee service narrative
playbook.action: Two separate reviews flag coffee quality and one details a genuinely bad macchiato experience; addressing staff training on espresso builds and menu communication closes the sentiment gap.

playbook.headline: Tag creators who are already filming
playbook.action: Twenty unique creators posted this quarter with no apparent coordination from the business; acknowledging even one reshare would open a direct line to the 598,874 people who already watched.
